completed_work: Added provider L0 invariant error collection for structured validation results.
Updated CLI validation output indirectly through provider_l0_invariant_validation_result without adding flags.

Added tests for collecting multiple invariant errors, preserving first-error exception behavior, collecting list-shape errors, avoiding duplicate malformed-item errors, and CLI JSON multiple-error output.
key_decisions: Keep validate_provider_l0_invariants as the first-error raising API for backward compatibility.
Use provider_l0_invariant_validation_result as the collect-all structured result API.
Do not change validation.l0_invariants schema version; continue using v1 with multiple errors.
next_actions: Consider L0 chunk JSON validation using the actual L0ChunkSummary.to_dict shape, malformed L0 store diagnostics, or guarded explicit L0 context-pack inclusion.
risks: Structured error mapping remains message-derived, so future ValueError message changes can affect code/path mapping.
The collector still covers only provider summary-layer invariants, not L0 chunk-file schema or citation/span validation.
The collector intentionally skips deeper checks for items whose cid/object shape is already invalid to avoid duplicate or cascading errors.
summary_text: AI-Lab added collect-all structured provider L0 invariant validation errors. provider_l0_invariant_validation_result now collects all detectable provider L0 summary invariant failures and returns version v1 with ok/errors, where errors preserve structured code, message, path, and optional chunk_id. validate_provider_l0_invariants remains unchanged as the first-error raising API. CLI JSON validation output now benefits from complete error lists without new flags or schema-version changes. This does not add new validation rules, does not validate L0 chunk files, and does not change prompt assembly, context packs, L0 creation/loading, or provider calls. Full suite passed with 251 tests.
